qa_pairs.question: What are some compatible SSH client software versions recommended for Windows users after the security updates?
qa_pairs.answer: For Windows, compatible SSH clients include MobaXterm Home Edition v11.1, PuTTY 0.72, and Windows Services for Linux (WSL) v1, specifically Ubuntu 18.04 (OpenSSH_7.6p1) and openSUSE Leap 15.1 (OpenSSH_7.9p1).

qa_pairs.question: Which SSH clients are compatible with the new configuration on macOS (OS X), and how can users check their client version?
qa_pairs.answer: Compatible SSH clients for macOS include OpenSSH 7.4p1 with OpenSSL 1.0.2k (Homebrew) and OpenSSH 7.9p1 with LibreSSL 2.7.3 (OS X 10.14.5). Users can determine their exact SSH client version on OS X using the command `ssh -V`.

qa_pairs.question: How can one remotely retrieve the SSH host key fingerprints for a cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To retrieve SSH host key fingerprints remotely, one can use the command `ssh-keyscan <hostname> | ssh-keygen -E md5 -l -f -` for MD5 fingerprints or `ssh-keyscan <hostname> | ssh-keygen -E sha256 -l -f -` for SHA256 fingerprints.
